commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Windows tray inbound pairing approval queue/coordinator/dialog, default-on setting, toasts, localization, docs, and tests for device/node pairing requests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a user bug reproduction, but yes for the review blockers: source inspection shows the type collision against current main and the missing ambiguous-fallback guard in the new queue path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor live gateway proof plus source fixes; repair automation should not be queued because it cannot supply the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: Add inbound pairing approval dialog (Mac-parity)
target.body:
## What & why

When another device or node requests pairing with the gateway, the Windows operator now gets a **focused approval dialog + awareness toast** — matching the macOS `DevicePairingApprovalPrompter` / `NodePairingApprovalPrompter` — instead of only a passive "Pending approvals" banner that you'd see only if you happened to be on the Connections page.

The plumbing was already mostly there: the client receives the gateway's real-time `device.pair.requested` / `node.pair.requested` push events, but only refreshed an in-memory list. This PR adds the missing "last mile": a proactive, transparent, security-conscious decision surface.

## What's in it

**Pure, unit-tested core (`OpenClaw.Connection`)**
- `PendingApproval` — unified device/node request model (ID truncation, `::ffff:` IP normalization, repair flag)
- `PairingApprovalQueue` — diff engine: Added/Resolved/Current, decided-suppression, own-node filter, dedup
- `PairingScopeDescriptions` — operator scope -> friendly label (ported from the Mac prompt)
- **32 new unit tests** in `OpenClaw.Connection.Tests`

**Orchestration + UI (`OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI`)**
- `PairingApprovalCoordinator` — queue + approve/reject RPCs, gated on `operator.pairing`/`admin` scope + the new setting, in-flight guards, and a **20s safety-net reconcile poll** that recovers requests dropped by the gateway''s `dropIfSlow=true` broadcast
- `PairingApprovalDialog` — code-built `WindowEx` (sidesteps the documented XAML-compiler bug); shows requester identity + **the operator scopes being granted**, kind-aware Approve label, **~1.5s anti-clickthrough delay**, queue navigation, Approve / Reject / Decide-later, localized + automation IDs
- Wiring: `GatewayService.PairListsChanged` feed, awareness + confirmation toasts, `review_pairing` toast action, reset on disconnect, own-node re-reconcile, shutdown teardown
- New `ShowPairingApprovalDialog` setting (default **on**); the in-page banner remains as the passive fallback

**Localization** across all 5 locales (en/fr/nl/zh-CN/zh-TW) and a `CONNECTION_ARCHITECTURE.md` update.

## Mac parity
Matched: dedicated dialog, identical friendly scope labels, identity + truncated ID + IP + repair badge, reassurance copy, push-driven queue, advance/close on resolve, post-decision notification, 3 actions. Added: anti-clickthrough delay (Mac has none). Intentionally **not** ported: silent SSH-reachability auto-approve (weakens the explicit-approval model; own node is already auto-approved).

## Security notes
- Approve is never the default button and is briefly disabled per request (anti click-through)
- Connection **and** approval scope are re-checked at decision time
- The dialog shows exactly which operator scopes are being granted, in plain language

## Reviews
Two adversarial rubber-duck passes (Claude Sonnet, then GPT-5.5). **Zero blockers** on final code; all surfaced issues fixed (reentrancy guard, reset-on-disconnect, own-node re-reconcile, decision-time scope re-check, non-reentrant poll guard + shutdown teardown).

## Validation
- `./build.ps1` — all 5 projects [pass]
- `OpenClaw.Connection.Tests` [pass] 298 (+32)
- `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` [pass] 2049
- `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` [pass] 959 (+1)

Per AGENTS.md, the runtime popup should be confirmed with a manual smoke test against a live gateway.
target.author: bkudiess
